workflow_rationale: Documentation site is a single-service build with no external dependencies. No multi-phase workflow needed since docs don't depend on backend being ready - docs can be built independently and reference the API.

phases.name: Project Setup
phases.description: Initialize Docusaurus project with TypeScript configuration

subtasks.description: Create docs directory structure and package.json

verification.command: cd docs && npm install --legacy-peer-deps 2>&1 | tail -5

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED (manual verification required): All 5 required files created. Files verified via Read tool: package.json, tsconfig.json, babel.config.js, docusaurus.config.ts, sidebars.ts. npm install blocked by sandbox - run manually: `cd docs && npm install --legacy-peer-deps`

subtasks.description: Configure Docusaurus with proper plugins and theme

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -10

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Enhanced docusaurus.config.ts with AuthMe branding. Added: baseUrl for proper routing, color mode (light/dark), enhanced navbar with logo and API Reference link, comprehensive footer with SDK/Resources/Community sections, Prism syntax highlighting for 7 languages, collapsible sidebar, announcement bar. Commit: 8b2c857. Verification blocked by missing node/npm - run manually: `npm run build`

phases.name: Core Documentation
phases.description: Create the core documentation pages and structure

subtasks.description: Create landing page with hero section and features overview
subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/src/pages/index.tsx with hero section (title, subtitle, CTA buttons, stats) and features overview (6 feature cards). Also created index.module.css for styling. Followed patterns from DashboardPage.tsx. Committed as 79f93a3.

subtasks.description: Create intro/overview documentation page

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/intro.md with comprehensive AuthMe overview including: What is AuthMe, Key Benefits (table), Features Overview (Authentication, Identity Management, Operations tables), Architecture diagram (Mermaid), Supported Standards, Tech Stack, When to Use AuthMe checklist, and Next Steps navigation links. Followed patterns from README.md. Build verification blocked by npm not available in sandbox environment. Manual verification required.

subtasks.description: Create quickstart guide with Docker Compose setup

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/quickstart.md with Docker Compose setup. File content verified via Read tool. Build verification blocked by npm/Docker sandbox restrictions.

subtasks.description: Create getting-started section with installation guide

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/getting-started/installation.md and docs/docs/getting-started/configuration.md. Files verified via Read tool. installation.md contains: Prerequisites table, Docker Hub setup, Docker Compose examples, From Source instructions, Database Options (PostgreSQL/MySQL/SQLite), Verifying Installation. configuration.md contains: Core Settings (Database, Server), Security Settings (API Auth, Webhook Encryption), Redis configuration, Rate Limiting, Reverse Proxy settings, Complete env var reference, Admin Console configuration notes. Both files follow patterns from README.md and .env.example. Build verification blocked by npm/npm not available - run manually: `cd docs && npm install --legacy-peer-deps && npm run build`

phases.name: API Reference
phases.description: Create API reference section with OpenAPI integration

subtasks.description: Create OpenAPI spec file from existing Swagger documentation

verification.command: cd docs && cat docs/api/openapi.yaml | head -50

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/api/openapi.yaml (2987 lines) containing OpenAPI 3.0.3 specification with: info section (title, description, version, contact, license), server configuration, security schemes (admin-api-key, bearerAuth), 8 tags (Realms, Users, Clients, Groups, Roles, OAuth, Tokens, Health), comprehensive paths for all API endpoints (Realms, Users, Clients, Groups, Roles, OAuth endpoints, Token introspection/revocation, UserInfo, Health checks), and all schemas (Realm, User, Client, Group, Role, TokenResponse, TokenIntrospectionResponse, OfflineSession, UserInfo, request/response schemas). Based on patterns from src/main.ts and src/oauth/oauth.controller.ts. File verified via Read tool - starts with 'openapi: 3.0.3' as expected.

subtasks.description: Create API reference page with Swagger UI integration

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created 6 API reference documentation files. Files verified via Read tool. index.mdx contains API overview, Swagger UI link, endpoints table. authentication.md covers token endpoint, all grant types, token introspection/revocation. oauth.md covers authorization, userinfo, logout endpoints, OpenID discovery. users.md covers CRUD operations, password reset, email verification, offline sessions. clients.md covers client management, secret regeneration, service accounts. realms.md covers realm CRUD, export/import, themes, test email. All files follow patterns from NestJS controllers with @ApiTags, @ApiOperation, @ApiResponse decorators. Build verification blocked by npm/npm not available - run manually: `cd docs && npm install --legacy-peer-deps && npm run build`

phases.name: SDK Guides
phases.description: Create SDK integration guides for all 6 platforms

subtasks.description: Create React SDK guide with AuthProvider and hooks examples

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/guides/sdks/react.mdx with comprehensive React SDK documentation. Covers: Installation, Quick Start, AuthProvider (inline config & pre-built client), useAuth hook, useUser hook, usePermissions hook, ProtectedRoute component, Token Refresh Strategies (rotation/eager/silent), Event System, TypeScript Support, and Next Steps navigation. Follows patterns from packages/authme-js/README.md and packages/authme-js/src/react.ts. Build verification blocked by sandbox restrictions - manual verification required: `cd docs && npm run build`

Follows patterns from packages/authme-nextjs/README.md. File verified via Glob - exists at docs/docs/guides/sdks/nextjs.mdx. Covers: Requirements table, Installation (Gradle, JitPack, AndroidManifest), Setup (redirect URI, intent filter, client creation), Login Flow (initiate login, handle redirect callback), Authentication State (isAuthenticated, getAccessToken, getUserInfo), Token Refresh (auto-refresh, manual refresh), Biometric Authentication (token gating, standalone prompt), Logout, Lifecycle Management (destroy), Error Handling (all exceptions table), ViewModel Example, and Next Steps navigation. subtasks.description: Create iOS SDK guide with Swift examples

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

Covers: Requirements table, Installation (Swift Package Manager), Setup (redirect URI registration, URL scheme configuration in Info.plist, client creation), Login Flow (ASWebAuthenticationSession), Authentication State (isAuthenticated, getAccessToken, getUserInfo), Token Refresh (auto-refresh, manual refresh), Biometric Authentication (Face ID/Touch ID gating, availability checking), Logout, Error Handling (all AuthMeError types table), Thread Safety (@MainActor), SwiftUI Example (complete ViewModel pattern), and Next Steps navigation. subtasks.description: Create authentication guide with OAuth flows

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/guides/authentication.mdx (900+ lines) with comprehensive OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect documentation. Covers: Supported OAuth flows table, Authorization Code + PKCE flow with complete sequence diagram, PKCE implementation in JS/Python/Kotlin, complete TypeScript examples, Client Credentials grant for service accounts, Device Authorization grant (RFC 8628), Token refresh with rotation, Step-up authentication with ACR values, Session management with concurrent session limits, Security best practices (PKCE, CSRF, token storage), and Error handling patterns. Follows patterns from oauth.controller.ts and auth.service.ts. Build verification blocked by sandbox restrictions (seccomp permission denied) - manual verification required: `cd docs && npm run build`

subtasks.description: Create authorization guide with RBAC and policies

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/guides/authorization.mdx (800+ lines) with comprehensive RBAC and ABAC policy documentation. Covers: Authorization overview with DENY-override algorithm, Roles and permissions (hierarchy, assignment, role-based permissions table), ABAC policy structure with all fields, condition operators (comparison, numeric, pattern), condition field paths using dot-notation, creating policies with curl examples and JSON policy examples, policy evaluation with ALLOW/DENY/default-deny response examples, testing single policies, client-scoped policies, policy priority management, CRUD operations (list, get, update, delete, disable), SDK integration in TypeScript/Python/Kotlin, best practices, and error handling. Follows patterns from src/authorization/authorization.service.ts, authorization.dto.ts, policy-engine.ts, and authorization.controller.ts. Build verification blocked by npm not available in sandbox environment - manual verification required: `cd docs && npm run build`

subtasks.description: Create MFA guide with TOTP setup

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/guides/mfa.mdx (599 lines) with comprehensive MFA/TOTP documentation. Covers: Supported methods table, TOTP setup flow with mermaid diagram, QR code display with React example, verification and activation, TOTP verification with standard login flow, recovery codes with usage/regeneration, MFA status checking (user self-check and admin check), admin MFA disabling (with MFA step-up requirements), TOTP configuration parameters, supported authenticator apps, security best practices, and error codes. Follows patterns from src/mfa/mfa.controller.ts and mfa.service.ts. Build verification blocked by npm not available in sandbox environment - manual verification required: `cd docs && npm run build`

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/configuration/environment-variables.md with comprehensive environment variables reference. Follows patterns from .env.example with detailed tables, examples, and warnings. File verified via Read tool (341 lines). Build verification blocked by sandbox restrictions (seccomp permission denied) - manual verification required: `cd docs && npm run build`

subtasks.description: Create configuration options reference

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/configuration/options.md (900+ lines) with comprehensive configuration options reference. Covers: Quick Reference by Category table, Realm Configuration (core settings, attributes, API endpoints), Security Configuration (authentication settings, OAuth flows), Theme Configuration (types, custom themes), Email Configuration (SMTP settings, connection URLs, test endpoints), Session Configuration (timeouts, format), Token Configuration (lifespans, revocation), Client Configuration (settings, URLs, flows), Password Policy (all policies), MFA Configuration (TOTP settings, recovery codes), Brute Force Protection (lockout behavior), Event Configuration (listeners, admin events), Client Scopes, Role Configuration (composite roles), User Federation (LDAP/Kerberos), Webhook Configuration (events, verification). Follows patterns from src/app.module.ts with detailed tables, examples, and curl requests. Build verification blocked by sandbox restrictions (seccomp permission denied) - manual verification required: `cd docs && npm run build`

phases.name: Deployment Guides
phases.description: Create deployment documentation for Docker, Kubernetes, and bare metal

subtasks.description: Create Docker Compose deployment guide

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/deployment/docker.mdx (439 lines) with comprehensive Docker Compose deployment documentation. Covers: Quick Start, Production Deployment (docker-compose.yml), Development Deployment (docker-compose.dev.yml), Database Options (PostgreSQL/MySQL/SQLite), Optional Redis Cache, Common Tasks (updating, backup/restore, logs, stopping), Troubleshooting, and Next Steps navigation. Follows patterns from docker-compose.yml and docker-compose.dev.yml. File verified via Read tool - contains frontmatter with id:docker, title, sidebar_position, description. Build verification blocked by npm not available in sandbox - manual verification required: `cd docs && npm install --legacy-peer-deps && npm run build`. Committed as 032bdf9.

subtasks.description: Create Kubernetes deployment guide

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/deployment/kubernetes.mdx (552 lines) with comprehensive Kubernetes deployment documentation. Covers: Prerequisites table, Quick Start (helm repo add, install from local chart), Installing from Local Chart (values file, secrets generation, install steps), Configuration Reference (image, service, ingress, resources, probes tables), Database Configuration (managed PostgreSQL, external PostgreSQL, Redis), High Availability (HPA, Pod Disruption Budget, Pod Anti-Affinity), Security Configuration (pod/container security contexts, NetworkPolicy example), Managing Deployments (upgrading, rolling back, uninstalling), Monitoring (health endpoints, logs, status commands), Troubleshooting (pod issues, ImagePullBackOff, database connection), and Next Steps navigation. Follows patterns from helm/authme/values.yaml, values.production.yaml, and deployment.yaml. File verified via Read tool - contains frontmatter with id:kubernetes, title, sidebar_position:2, description. Build verification blocked by npm not available in sandbox - manual verification required: `cd docs && npm install --legacy-peer-deps && npm run build`

subtasks.description: Create bare metal deployment guide

verification.command: cd docs && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'Success'

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: Created docs/docs/deployment/bare-metal.mdx (500+ lines) with comprehensive bare metal deployment documentation. Covers: Prerequisites table, Quick Start (setup script), Installing from Source (Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis), Building AuthMe (dependencies, configuration, database setup, build), Systemd Service Setup (service file, user creation, enabling service, management), Reverse Proxy Configuration (Nginx, Caddy with SSL), Database Backups (cron, backup script), Firewall Configuration (UFW, firewalld), Monitoring (health checks, Prometheus metrics, logrotate), Updating AuthMe, Security Hardening (permissions, Fail2Ban), and Troubleshooting. Follows patterns from README.md#from-source and other deployment guides (docker.mdx, kubernetes.mdx). File verified via Read tool - contains frontmatter with id:bare-metal, title, sidebar_position:3, description. Build verification blocked by sandbox restrictions (seccomp permission denied) - manual verification required: `cd docs && npm run build`. Committed as part of subtask-7-3.

parallel_groups.reason: Both depend only on phase-1-setup, no file conflicts
All depend on phase-2-content, no file conflicts between SDK/guides/deployment/migration

summary.startup_command: cd docs && npm install --legacy-peer-deps && npm run start

acceptance_criteria: All documentation pages build without errors
All links between pages work correctly
Search functionality works across all pages
SDK guides contain working code examples

verification_steps.name: Dev Server Start
verification_steps.command: cd docs && timeout 30 npm run start || true

verification_strategy.reasoning: Low risk documentation-only build - no security concerns, simple static site generation
